mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5% indicates tightening labor markets, which drives corporate profit growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 5.0 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and rising housing starts (HOUST) signals improving real demand conditions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows or 60d returns show negative correlation with combined CPIAUCSL decline and HOUST rise.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 118.0 signals strengthening real sector activity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds 118.0 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in the federal funds rate above 3.0% signals monetary tightening, which increases equity discount rates and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in real sales (RSAFS) and rising housing starts (HOUST) signals conflicting demand dynamics, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below -0.05 over 3-year rolling windows or 20d returns show positive correlation with combined RSAFS decline and HOUST rise.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 150k signals strong labor market conditions, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 50%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) below 1.5% signals deflationary risks, which drives central bank policy accommodation and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL falls below 1.5 despite the signal.
